action.reasoning: Observation: Training completed 5 epochs successfully and saved checkpoints/best.pt, but predict.py crashed with a shape mismatch during normalization. Analysis: The test input has shape [B,3,2,32,64], but mean/std were viewed as [1,2,1,1] which does not broadcast to 5D (fails at dim 1 vs 3). Also normalization happened after reshaping to [B,6,32,64], which changes the channel count. Decision: Fix predict.py to view mean/std as [1,1,2,1,1] so they broadcast to the 5D input, normalize first, then reshape to [B,6,32,64]. Run a local dry-run with dummy test inputs from val_data to verify, then immediately submit eval.
action.command: mkdir -p /tmp/test_eval && cp /data/norm_stats.json /tmp/test_eval/ && cp /data/lat.npy /tmp/test_eval/ && python -c "import numpy as np; data=np.load('/data/val_data.npy'); windows=np.stack([data[i:i+3] for i in range(10)]); np.save('/tmp/test_eval/test_inputs.npy', windows)" && python predict.py --data_path /tmp/test_eval --checkpoint checkpoints/best.pt --output /tmp/test_eval/out.json && echo 'LOCAL TEST PASSED'
